'''Lachlan Walmsley''' (born 12 June 1998) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|fb}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] in the [[2022 RFL Championship]] and [[Whitehaven RLFC|Whitehaven]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/lachlan-walmsley-48900.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Walmsley was born in [[Merriwa, New South Wales|Merriwa]], [[New South Wales, Australia]]. He is of [[Scotland|Scottish]] descent.<ref>[https://www.sconeadvocate.com.au/story/7480848/lachlan-walmsley-signs-for-halifax-panthers-in-uk-championship/ Merriwa born Walmsley signs for Halifax Panthers in the UK's Betfred Championship]</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
He came through the youth system at the [[Newcastle Knights]] and reserve grade for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], and played for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales under 18s]] in the [[State of Origin]] at youth level.<ref>[https://www.playlockr.com/rugby-league-profiles/lachlan-walsmley LACHLAN WALMSLEY]</ref><ref>[https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/halifax-panthers-win-race-to-sign-winger-walmsley-3421496 Halifax Panthers win race to sign winger Walmsley]</ref><ref>[https://nothingbutleague.com/player/lachlan-walmsley/ Nothing But League profile]</ref>

===Whitehaven RLFC===
Walmsley was the top point scorer for [[Whitehaven RLFC|Whitehaven]] in their [[2021 RFL League 1|2021 season]].<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/walmsley-set-for-giant-super-league-move-43631/ Walmsley set for giant Super League move]</ref><ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lachlan-walmsley-louis-jouffret-halifax-2024/ Overseas duo sign two-year deals at Halifax]</ref>

===Halifax Panthers===
He joined [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] ahead of the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 season]] and was the top try-scorer for the [[Halifax Panthers|Panthers]] in their 2022 season<ref>[https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/7873638/rugby-league-world-cup-beckons-for-in-form-walmsley/ Rugby League: Upper Hunter product Lachlan Walmsley sits second on Championship try-scoring leaderboard, World Cup shot for Scotland within reach]</ref> and named (jointly with [[Dean Parata]]) as the 2023 Championship Player of the Year at the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] annual awards.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bevan-french-sinead-peach-lewis-king-scoop-top-accolades-2023-rugby-league-awards |title=Bevan French, Sinead Peach, Lewis King scoop top accolades at 2023 Rugby League Awards |website=Love Rugby League |date=10 October 2023 |access-date=13 August 2024}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 7 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 Nov 2023|title=Wakefield Trinity swoop for Championship Player of the Year for undisclosed fee: 'A Super League player in the making'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-swoop-for-championship-player-of-the-year-lachlan-walmsley-for-undisclosed-fee}}</ref>

On 9 July 2025, Walmsley signed a new one-year contract extension with Wakefield Trinity to remain at the club through the end of the 2026 season.<ref name="Trinity2025">{{cite web |title=Walmsley To Stay With The Trin |url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/walmsley-to-stay-with-the-trin/ |website=Wakefield Trinity RLFC |date=9 July 2025 |access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Henshaw |first=Callum |title=Lachlan Walmsley Commits to Wakefield Trinity with New One-Year Deal |url=https://rugbyleaguezone.com/lachlan-walmsley-commits-to-wakefield-trinity-with-new-one-year-deal-413975/ |website=Rugby League Zone |date=10 July 2025 |access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>

===International career===
In 2021 Walmsley made his international début for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]].<ref>[https://cumbriacrack.com/2021/10/20/lachlan-walmsley-called-up-for-scotland/ Lachlan Walmsley called up for Scotland]</ref><ref>[https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/rookies-in-line-for-scotland-rl-debut-c-4282903 Rookies in line for Scotland RL debut]</ref>

In 2022 Walmsley was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Scotland|Scotland squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021]</ref> scoring his first try for {{rlnt|Scotland}} in the 28-4 defeat to {{rlnt|Italy}} on 16 October 2022.{{citation needed|date=January 2024}}
